message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Background</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>During the COVID-19 pandemic, serological tests to screen populations have provided better estimates of the cumulative incidence of infection. This study evaluated the seroprevalence of SARS-CoV-2 in agricultural workers in rural Guatemala, their COVID-19 vaccine uptake and vaccination attitudes.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>A cross-sectional study was undertaken from August to November of 2021, in agricultural workers at a sugar plantation in Guatemala. A questionnaire was used to collect demographic, previous COVID-19 infection, vaccination, and attitudes toward vaccination. Serological testing was performed to detect SARS-CoV-2 IgM and IgG.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Of the 4,343 study participants, 1,279 (29.4%) were seropositive for SARS-CoV-2 compared to 2.3% who reported previous COVID-19 infection. COVID-19 vaccine coverage was 85% for the first dose and 21.9% for second dose. Vaccine refusal was 0.6%, and 13.9% expressed some degree of vaccine hesitancy. Vaccine hesitant workers or those refusing were less likely to have had the COVID-19 vaccine. Main reasons to get the vaccine were to protect family, coworkers, and community.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusion</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Agricultural workers in countries like Guatemala have suffered a high incidence of asymptomatic and undetected SARS-CoV-2 infection. Most have received the COVID-19 vaccine, but there are moderate degrees of vaccine hesitancy that require better public health information to overcome it.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: SARS-CoV-2 seroprevalence, vaccination, and hesitancy in agricultural workers in Guatemala

reference.unstructured: Pan American Health Organization. COVID-19 Situation Report no. 67. Vol. 67. Report. 2021. 12/31/2021. https://www.paho.org/en/documents/covid-19-pahowho-response-report-67-31-december-2021
Food and Drug Administration. Serology Test Evaluation Report for “STANDARD Q COVID-19 IgM/IgG Duo” from SD BIOSENSOR, Inc. 06/21/2020 2020;
